text.p:
A free-text input allows you to type in free text, e.g. your complete working or mathematical proof. You will see an immediate preview, with mathematics displayed.
You can type markdown text.
You can type AsciiMath between backticks for mathematics: `...`.
You can include LaTeX between brackets, \(...\) for inline mathematics and \[...\] displayed mathematics.
If activated by the teacher, calculations between {@...@} will be automatically evaluated.
AsciiMath has two forms. Inline AsciiMath starts and ends with a backtick, e.g. `sum_(k=1)^oo 1/(n^2)=pi^2/6`.

Displayed AsciiMath starts and ends with a backtick on an empty line, and mathematics lines in between. E.g. ` x^2-5x+6=0 (x-3)(x-2)=0 ` AsciiMath is considerably simpler than LaTeX and should be adequate for many common expressions.
